verbatim_quote: Benerro has sent forth the word from Volantis. Her coming is the fulfillment of an ancient prophecy. From smoke and salt was she born to make the world anew. She is Azor Ahai returned …
Behold! A sign was promised, and now a sign is seen! Behold Lightbringer! Azor Ahai has come again! All hail the Warrior of Light! All hail the Son of Fire!
The sword is wrong, she has to know that . . . light without heat . . . an empty glamor . . . the sword is wrong, and the false light can only lead us deeper into darkness, Sam.
Only death can pay for life.
The smoke was from the fire that devoured Summerhall on the day of his birth, the salt from the tears shed for those who died.
note: Aemon discusses Rhaegar's birth amid smoke and salt, the prophecy signs

verbatim_quote: Sometimes when Victarion closed his hand the skin would split and smoke, yet the arm was stronger than it had ever been.
note: Victarion's burned hand is described as smoking

verbatim_quote: his sword burning in his right hand
note: Beric Dondarrion's sword described as burning

verbatim_quote: find a better match for her than Ramsay Snow
note: Ramsay Snow appears in letter context; both he and Jon share the Snow bastard surname

verbatim_quote: Lord Aenar's maiden daughter Daenys, known forever afterward as Daenys the Dreamer, had foreseen the destruction of Valyria
note: Targaryen prophecy tradition established via Daenys the Dreamer's visions

verbatim_quote: Daemon dreamed that a dragon would be born at Whitewalls, and it was.
note: Daemon Blackfyre's prophetic dream in D&E novellas; confirms Targaryen prophetic tradition

verbatim_quote: she named them Aegon, Rhaenys, and Visenya
note: Names connecting three-headed dragon sigil (Aegon, Rhaenys, Visenya) to Rhaegar's children; Targaryen sigil lore established via these name parallels

verbatim_quote: In this dread hour a warrior shall draw from the fire a burning sword. And that sword shall be Lightbringer, the Red Sword of Heroes
When I was a boy, I dreamt that I could fly. When I woke, I couldn't . . . or so the maester said. But what if he lied?
to seize the body of another man was the worst abomination of all
note: Varamyr passage establishes bodysnatching/possession as precedent; beat grounds underlying scene of magical possession possibility

verbatim_quote: If Joffrey should die . . . what is the life of one bastard boy against a kingdom?

note: Meera confronts Coldhands; fuller context shows he refuses to reveal his identity

verbatim_quote: The right eye was open. The pupil burned blue. It saw.
note: Waymar Royce killed by Others and becomes wight with blue-burning eyes; grounds theory of Others killing and turning individuals

verbatim_quote: Some say he was a Bolton,
note: Old Nan directly suggests Night's King might have been a Bolton; connects House Bolton to supernatural narrative
